plant_disease.name: apple apple scab
plant_disease.symptoms: Olive-green to black spots on leaves, which become velvety and brown over time. Fruit develops circular brown/black spots that can crack.
plant_disease.management: Prune infected branches, clear fallen leaves, and apply copper-based fungicides during the green tip phase.

plant_disease.name: apple black rot
plant_disease.symptoms: Reddish-brown spots on leaves (frogeye leaf spot), sunken black cankers on branches, and dry rot on fruit with concentric zones.
plant_disease.management: Remove dead wood and mummified fruit, prune cankers, and apply protective fungicides during early season.

plant_disease.name: apple cedar apple rust
plant_disease.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Tubular spore cups develop on leaf undersides and fruit.
plant_disease.management: Remove nearby cedar trees (alternative host), plant rust-resistant cultivars, and apply fungicides at bud break.

plant_disease.name: apple healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Leaves are lush green, firm, and free of blemishes. Tree shows good growth and fruit development.
plant_disease.management: Continue regular watering, pruning, fertilizing, and monitoring for pests.

plant_disease.name: blueberry healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Deep green leaves with healthy stems and abundant flowering/fruiting. No visible leaf spots.
plant_disease.management: Maintain acidic soil pH (4.5-5.5), mulch root zone, and provide regular moisture.

plant_disease.name: cherry including sour powdery mildew
plant_disease.symptoms: White, powdery fungal patches on leaves and young shoots, causing leaf curling and stunted growth.
plant_disease.management: Prune to improve airflow, avoid overhead watering, and apply sulfur-based fungicides.

plant_disease.name: cherry including sour healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Foliage is green and vibrant. No signs of fungal leaf spots, powdery residue, or cankers.
plant_disease.management: Keep soil well-drained, prune annually for sunlight penetration, and apply fertilizer in spring.

plant_disease.name: corn maize cercospora leaf spot gray leaf spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Long, rectangular, grayish-brown lesions running parallel to leaf veins, eventually blending to kill entire leaves.
plant_disease.management: Use resistant corn hybrids, practice crop rotation, tillage to bury residues, and apply foliar fungicides.

plant_disease.name: corn maize common rust
plant_disease.symptoms: Powdery, reddish-brown pustules on both upper and lower leaf surfaces, causing early leaf death.
plant_disease.management: Plant rust-resistant hybrids and apply fungicides early if disease pressure is high.

plant_disease.name: corn maize northern leaf blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Large, cigar-shaped, grayish-green to tan lesions on leaves, starting on lower leaves.
plant_disease.management: Rotate crops, till crop residue, plant resistant hybrids, and apply fungicides.

plant_disease.name: corn maize healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Robust stalks with deep green, long leaves and healthy ear development. No lesions or rust pustules.
plant_disease.management: Maintain balanced nitrogen levels, manage weeds, and ensure proper irrigation.

plant_disease.name: grape black rot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small round brown spots on leaves. Berries shrivel into hard, black, wrinkled mummies.
plant_disease.management: Prune vines to open the canopy, remove all mummified berries, and apply fungicides from bud break through bloom.

plant_disease.name: grape esca black measles
plant_disease.symptoms: Tiger-stripe patterns on leaves (interveinal yellowing/browning). Berries develop dark spots or shrivel.
plant_disease.management: Protect pruning wounds, remove infected wood during dry weather, and use wound sealants.

plant_disease.name: grape leaf blight isariopsis leaf spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark brown, irregular spots on leaves, which may dry up and drop prematurely.
plant_disease.management: Apply copper fungicides, clean up fallen leaves in autumn, and improve canopy ventilation.

plant_disease.name: grape healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Vibrant green leaves, strong vines, and clean grape clusters free of shriveled berries.
plant_disease.management: Prune during dormancy, fertilize based on soil tests, and maintain good weed control.

plant_disease.name: orange haunglongbing citrus greening
plant_disease.symptoms: Yellow mottled leaves, stunted growth, and small, misshapen, green, bitter-tasting fruit.
plant_disease.management: Control the Asian citrus psyllid vector, remove infected trees, and apply nutritional sprays to support vigor.

plant_disease.name: peach bacterial spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown and drop out (shot-hole). Fruit shows dark spots and cracks.
plant_disease.management: Plant resistant varieties, avoid excess nitrogen, and apply copper sprays in early spring.

plant_disease.name: peach healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Clean, unblemished leaves and smooth-skinned fruit. Stems show healthy new growth.
plant_disease.management: Thin fruit to prevent overload, prune for airflow, and monitor for borers.

plant_disease.name: pepper bell bacterial spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small, circular, raised spots on leaves and fruit, causing leaf yellowing and defoliation.
plant_disease.management: Use pathogen-free seed, rotate crops, avoid overhead watering, and apply copper bactericides.

plant_disease.name: pepper bell healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark green, upright plants with thick leaves and glossy, firm peppers.
plant_disease.management: Keep soil evenly moist, fertilize with balanced nutrients, and stake plants for support.

plant_disease.name: potato early blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark spots with concentric rings (target board pattern) on older leaves. Lower leaves turn yellow.
plant_disease.management: Maintain high plant vigor, rotate crops, remove crop debris, and apply protective fungicides.

plant_disease.name: potato late blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Dark, water-soaked leaf lesions with white mold on undersides in wet weather. Tubers rot.
plant_disease.management: Plant certified disease-free seed, destroy volunteer potato plants, and apply preventive fungicides.

plant_disease.name: potato healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Lush green canopy and healthy vine growth. No lesions or mold present.
plant_disease.management: Hill soil around plants, water at the base, and monitor closely during cool, wet periods.

plant_disease.name: raspberry healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Healthy green leaves, upright canes, and bright, sweet fruit clusters.
plant_disease.management: Prune old fruiting canes, mulch to conserve moisture, and ensure good drainage.

plant_disease.name: soybean healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Bushy, deep green foliage with healthy pod development and no leaf spots or rust.
plant_disease.management: Ensure proper spacing, rotate with corn or wheat, and inoculate seeds with rhizobia.

plant_disease.name: squash powdery mildew
plant_disease.symptoms: White, powdery fungal spots spreading over leaves and stems, causing leaves to dry and wither.
plant_disease.management: Ensure full sun exposure, prune for airflow, and apply potassium bicarbonate or neem oil sprays.

plant_disease.name: strawberry leaf scorch
plant_disease.symptoms: Purplish spots on leaves that enlarge and turn dark brown, giving leaves a scorched appearance.
plant_disease.management: Plant resistant cultivars, renovate beds after harvest, and avoid overhead watering.

plant_disease.name: strawberry healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Bright green leaves, clean white flowers, and plump, red berries.
plant_disease.management: Mulch with straw, remove runners to maintain spacing, and water consistently.

plant_disease.name: tomato bacterial spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small, dark, greasy-looking spots on leaves and stems. Fruit develops raised, scabby spots.
plant_disease.management: Use clean seed, rotate crops, avoid overhead watering, and apply copper-based sprays.

plant_disease.name: tomato early blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Concentric black spots on older leaves, leading to yellowing and drop. Stem lesions can girdle seedlings.
plant_disease.management: Mulch soil, prune lower leaves, rotate crops, and apply chlorothalonil or copper fungicides.

plant_disease.name: tomato late blight
plant_disease.symptoms: Rapidly spreading large dark brown lesions on leaves and fruit with white fuzzy mold underneath in humid conditions.
plant_disease.management: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately, choose resistant varieties, and apply preventive copper sprays.

plant_disease.name: tomato leaf mold
plant_disease.symptoms: Pale green to yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces, with olive-green velvety mold on leaf undersides.
plant_disease.management: Reduce humidity in greenhouses, improve airflow, and use resistant cultivars.

plant_disease.name: tomato septoria leaf spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Numerous small, circular spots with dark borders and grey centers on lower leaves, causing severe defoliation.
plant_disease.management: Avoid overhead watering, destroy infected crop residue, mulch, and apply fungicides.

plant_disease.name: tomato spider mites two spotted spider mite
plant_disease.symptoms: Fine yellow speckling on leaves, followed by bronzing, drying, and fine webbing on leaves.
plant_disease.management: Spray with insecticidal soap, introduce predatory mites, and keep plants well-watered.

plant_disease.name: tomato target spot
plant_disease.symptoms: Small brown spots with concentric circles (targets) on leaves and fruit, causing premature leaf drop.
plant_disease.management: Space plants well, prune lower foliage, and apply protective fungicides.

plant_disease.name: tomato yellow leaf curl virus
plant_disease.symptoms: Stunted growth, leaves curl upwards and inwards, turning yellow at margins. Reduced fruit set.
plant_disease.management: Control silverleaf whitefly vectors with row covers, yellow sticky traps, or insecticides, and plant resistant varieties.

plant_disease.name: tomato mosaic virus
plant_disease.symptoms: Mottling light and dark green patterns on leaves, leaf distortion (shoestringing), and uneven ripening of fruit.
plant_disease.management: Sanitize tools, wash hands with soap, plant resistant cultivars, and remove infected plants.

plant_disease.name: tomato healthy
plant_disease.symptoms: Robust vines, dark green leaves, and vibrant red fruit with no spots, curling, or molds.
plant_disease.management: Prune suckers, stake plants, water deeply at the base, and fertilize regularly.

plant_disease.name: background
plant_disease.symptoms: The image does not show a recognizable plant leaf, or is out of focus.
plant_disease.management: Please point the camera closer and focus on an infected plant leaf.
